title: galium palustre var. elatius
intro: Galium palustre var. elatius, also known as the tall marsh bedstraw, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Galium palustre var. elatius include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Galium palustre var. elatius include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
